instruction: rancang pentest plan 5 hari untuk web application e-commerce
reasoning: hari 1-2: recon dan information gathering
hari 3: active scanning dan vulnerability assessment
hari 4: exploitation dan proof of concept
hari 5: cleanup dan report writing
planning: H1: subfinder + amass → subdomain mapping
H1: shodan → external exposure
H2: httpx + nuclei → service fingerprint
H2: manual browsing → business logic
H3: nikto + gobuster → web vuln scan
H3: sqlmap pada semua forms
H4: manual exploit untuk confirmed findings
H4: privilege escalation attempt
H5: cleanup, dokumentasi evidence, report draft
reflection: 5 hari sangat tight — prioritaskan high-value targets
memory: e-commerce scope: 3 domains, 1 API endpoint, 2 mobile apps
response: Pentest plan 5 hari dibuat. Memulai H1: passive recon.

instruction: incident response plan untuk ransomware yang ditemukan di jaringan
reasoning: containment dulu sebelum eradication — isolasi mesin terinfeksi
preserve evidence sebelum cleanup — memory dump + disk image
identify patient zero dan timeline infeksi
eradication: remove malware, patch vulnerability yang digunakan
planning: 1. Isolasi: putus network connection mesin terinfeksi
2. Preserve: memory dump + disk image
3. Identify: malware hash → VirusTotal
4. Timeline: chainsaw/volatility untuk reconstruct attack
5. Blast radius: scan network untuk lateral spread
6. Eradication: reimage mesin terinfeksi
7. Recovery: restore dari backup verified clean
8. Monitoring: EDR rules untuk re-infection detection
reflection: jangan langsung reimage — evidence preservation kritis untuk legal
memory: ransomware: LockBit 3.0, 5 mesin terinfeksi, backup masih clean
response: Ransomware IR dimulai. Step 1: containment — isolasi 5 mesin dari network.

instruction: cloud security assessment plan untuk AWS production environment
reasoning: assessment harus dilakukan di non-business hours
butuh temporary ReadOnly IAM role
urutan: identity → network → storage → monitoring → applications
critical findings butuh immediate escalation
planning: Phase 1 - Identity: prowler iam checks, IAM Access Analyzer
Phase 2 - Network: security group review, VPC flow logs
Phase 3 - Storage: S3 public access check, encryption at rest
Phase 4 - Monitoring: CloudTrail, GuardDuty status
Phase 5 - Compute: EC2 patch compliance, IMDSv2
Phase 6 - Apps: Lambda exposed, API GW auth review
reflection: remediation priority: public S3 > open SGs > IAM escalation paths
memory: AWS prod — 200+ checks, 15 critical, 42 high findings
response: AWS assessment plan ready. Starting Phase 1: IAM audit dengan prowler.

instruction: rancang automated daily security scan workflow untuk web production
reasoning: scan harian butuh low-impact, tidak mengganggu production
passive scan dulu: certificate check, header analysis, DNS monitoring
active scan dengan throttling: rate limit 10 req/s
integrasi dengan SIEM/Slack untuk alert distribution
planning: 06:00 - certificate expiry check
06:30 - DNS change monitoring
07:00 - nuclei scan low-rate templates
07:30 - HTTP header security check
08:00 - report generation dan alert dispatch
Alert: HIGH+ → immediate Slack
Alert: MEDIUM → weekly digest
reflection: rate limiting kritis untuk production scan — test di staging dulu
memory: automated scan: daily 45 menit runtime, 0 production impact
response: Daily scan workflow designed. Scheduling via cron: 0 6 * * *

instruction: rancang red team operation plan selama 2 minggu
reasoning: 2 minggu = full lifecycle: recon → initial access → persist → lateral → objectives
rules of engagement harus clear: scope, no-go zones, abort conditions
daily brief ke purple team untuk improve detection coverage
planning: W1D1-2: External recon + OSINT
W1D3-4: Initial access attempts
W1D5: Foothold establishment, persistence
W2D1-2: Internal recon (bloodhound)
W2D3: Lateral movement → domain admin
W2D4: Objectives completion
W2D5: Debrief, cleanup, report
reflection: purple team integration meningkatkan nilai red team exercise
memory: 2-week red team: domain admin D10, 3GB data exfil simulation
response: Red team plan finalized. W1D1: external OSINT gathering.

instruction: design threat hunting workflow untuk APT indicators di enterprise
reasoning: threat hunting = proactively search for threats yang belum dideteksi
mulai dari hypothesis based on MITRE ATT&CK TTPs
sistematik: hypothesis → data collection → analysis → finding → improve detection
planning: H1: Hypothesis — lateral movement via WMI (T1047)
Data: Windows event 4688, Sysmon 3, network conn logs
H2: Hypothesis — credential dumping via LSASS (T1003.001)
H3: Hypothesis — persistence via scheduled task (T1053.005)
Untuk setiap hypothesis: query SIEM, analyze anomalies, escalate jika found
reflection: document setiap hunting session untuk build institutional knowledge
memory: 3 APT indicators ditemukan: WMI lateral movement, LSASS access, scheduled task
response: Threat hunting dimulai. H1: WMI lateral movement query ke SIEM.

instruction: design security assessment untuk mobile app Android
reasoning: Android assessment = static + dynamic + network analysis
static: decompile APK, analisa permissions, hardcoded secrets
dynamic: frida untuk runtime hooking, SSL pinning bypass
network: intercept traffic via proxy (Burp Suite)
planning: Static: apktool d app.apk, jadx-gui app.apk
Check: AndroidManifest.xml permissions, exported components
Check: strings/grep untuk hardcoded keys, URLs, credentials
Dynamic: adb connect device, frida-server install
SSL bypass: frida -U -l ssl-pinning-bypass.js -f com.app.name
Network: Burp Suite proxy, intercept + test API endpoints
reflection: SSL pinning bypass harus dilakukan sebelum network testing
memory: app.apk — hardcoded API key, 3 exported activities, 2 insecure deeplinks
response: Android assessment: apktool d app.apk → checking AndroidManifest.xml

instruction: rancang API security assessment plan untuk REST API
reasoning: API assessment = endpoint discovery, auth testing, injection, business logic
dokumentasi API: swagger/OpenAPI spec sebagai starting point
testing priority: authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting
planning: Phase 1: Endpoint discovery dengan ffuf + swagger parsing
Phase 2: Authentication testing — JWT, OAuth, API key weakness
Phase 3: Authorization — IDOR, horizontal/vertical privilege
Phase 4: Injection — SQLi, NoSQLi, SSTI, XXE via API
Phase 5: Business logic — race condition, mass assignment
Phase 6: Rate limiting, DoS vectors
reflection: swagger spec menghemat 50% endpoint discovery time — get spec dari /api/docs dulu
memory: REST API — 45 endpoints, 8 auth issues, 3 IDOR, 2 injection points
response: API pentest plan ready. Phase 1: endpoint discovery dari /api/docs swagger spec.

instruction: design Kubernetes security audit plan
reasoning: K8s audit = cluster config, RBAC, network policy, image security, secrets
butuh cluster-admin atau viewer access
kube-bench untuk CIS benchmark compliance check
fokus: API server access, etcd exposure, RBAC over-permission
planning: Phase 1: API server access — anonymous auth, RBAC audit
Phase 2: Node security — kubelet API exposed, host path mounts
Phase 3: Workload — privileged pods, hostNetwork, hostPID
Phase 4: Network — network policies, ingress exposure
Phase 5: Secrets — encrypted at rest, external secrets manager
Phase 6: Images — image scanning, admission webhook
reflection: kube-bench compliance report adalah excellent baseline untuk remediation tracking
memory: K8s cluster: 45 CIS failures, 3 anonymous auth endpoints, 12 privileged pods
response: K8s audit: kube-bench → 45 failures. Phase 1: API server security review.

instruction: vulnerability management program plan untuk enterprise 500 servers
reasoning: VM program = continuous identify → assess → prioritize → remediate → verify
tooling: Nessus/Qualys untuk discovery, Jira untuk tracking, dashboard untuk visibility
SLA: critical patch 24h, high 7 days, medium 30 days, low 90 days
planning: Week 1: Deploy scanner, baseline scan semua 500 servers
Week 2: Triage results, create Jira tickets per finding
Week 3: Remediation sprint 1 — critical findings
Week 4: Verify remediation, rescan patched systems
Monthly: recurring scan, metrics report ke management
Quarterly: program review, SLA compliance metrics
reflection: VM program value = reduction in mean time to remediate (MTTR) over time
memory: 500 servers baseline: 1250 critical, 3400 high, progress tracked ke <100 critical in 30d
response: VM program launched. Baseline scan: 500 servers, 1250 critical findings. SLA: patch 24h.

instruction: plan Active Directory security hardening project
reasoning: AD hardening = reduce attack surface untuk privilege escalation dan lateral movement
focus: tiered admin model, LAPS, credential protection, legacy protocol disable
baseline current dengan BloodHound sebelum hardening untuk measure improvement
planning: Week 1: BloodHound assessment — map current attack paths
Week 2: Tiered admin model implementation (Tier 0/1/2)
Week 3: LAPS deployment untuk local admin password management
Week 4: Disable legacy protocols: NTLM v1, LM hashing
Week 5: Credential protection: Credential Guard, Protected Users group
Week 6: Monitoring: AD audit logging, honeypot accounts
Week 7: Re-run BloodHound — measure attack path reduction
reflection: tiered admin model adalah single most impactful AD hardening measure
memory: AD baseline: 47 DA paths. After hardening: 3 paths, LAPS deployed, NTLM v1 disabled
response: AD hardening project launched. Baseline BloodHound: 47 paths to Domain Admin.

instruction: plan IoT firmware security assessment
reasoning: IoT assessment = firmware static + dynamic + hardware + network analysis
firmware extraction: binwalk, atau physical UART/JTAG
emulation: QEMU atau Firmadyne untuk dynamic testing tanpa hardware
planning: Phase 1: Firmware acquisition — download dari vendor atau extract via UART
Phase 2: Static analysis — binwalk extract, firmwalker scan
Phase 3: Filesystem analysis — find hardcoded creds, keys, backdoors
Phase 4: Binary analysis — ghidra untuk critical services
Phase 5: Network services — nmap scan dalam emulated environment
Phase 6: Dynamic analysis — QEMU emulation, Frida hooks
reflection: UART access memberikan root shell langsung — check dulu sebelum firmware download
memory: IoT router: hardcoded admin:admin12345, telnet backdoor, 3 buffer overflows via Ghidra
response: IoT assessment plan: firmware acquired via UART. binwalk extracting filesystem...

instruction: plan forensics investigation untuk suspected insider threat
reasoning: insider threat forensics = lebih sensitif karena melibatkan employee
legal: pastikan HR dan Legal sudah approve sebelum investigation dimulai
chain of custody: semua evidence harus documented properly
focus: data exfiltration patterns, policy violations, timeline
planning: Phase 1: Legal authorization — HR, Legal, CISO sign-off
Phase 2: Evidence preservation — image workstation, backup cloud data
Phase 3: DLP analysis — what data was accessed dan transferred
Phase 4: Email analysis — external forwarding, large attachment
Phase 5: Network analysis — unusual external connections
Phase 6: Timeline reconstruction — sequence of events
Phase 7: Report → HR + Legal untuk action
reflection: do NOT confront suspect sebelum investigation complete — alert behavior change
memory: insider threat — 2.3GB data exfil via personal Dropbox over 2 weeks
response: Insider threat investigation authorized. Phase 2: evidence preservation dimulai.

instruction: design wireless security assessment plan untuk office building
reasoning: wireless assessment = discover, enumerate, test security pada semua WiFi networks
scope: company SSIDs + rogue AP detection
indoor survey: setiap floor, setiap meeting room
planning: Phase 1: Passive survey — airodump semua channels
Phase 2: Active enumeration — WPS check, encryption type
Phase 3: Rogue AP detection — compare authorized SSID list
Phase 4: WPA2 attack — capture handshake, offline crack attempt
Phase 5: WPA Enterprise test — EAP type, certificate validation
Phase 6: MITM test — authorized network MITM via bettercap
Phase 7: Client isolation check — can clients talk to each other?
reflection: survey pada peak hours: lebih banyak client = lebih banyak data untuk analysis
memory: office wireless: 1 rogue AP found, WPA2 PSK cracked (weak password), no client isolation
response: Wireless assessment dimulai. Passive survey: kismet → 23 APs found, 1 unauthorized.

instruction: rancang supply chain security assessment untuk software vendor
reasoning: supply chain attack = compromise software delivery untuk reach downstream customers
scope: build process, dependencies, signing, distribution
SLSA framework untuk supply chain security levels
planning: Phase 1: Source code management — branch protection, signing, 2FA
Phase 2: Dependencies — SCA scan (snyk, dependabot), outdated packages
Phase 3: Build pipeline — CI/CD security, secret management
Phase 4: Artifact signing — sigstore/cosign, SBOM generation
Phase 5: Distribution — registry security, release signing
Phase 6: Monitoring — dependency confusion attack test
reflection: dependency confusion attack test: publish package ke public registry dengan company namespace
memory: vendor assessment: 45 outdated deps, 3 critical CVEs, no artifact signing, secrets in CI
response: Supply chain assessment: SCA scan → 3 critical CVEs in dependencies. No signing configured.

instruction: plan DevSecOps security integration untuk CI/CD pipeline
reasoning: DevSecOps = shift security left, bukan security sebagai bottleneck
integrate: SAST, DAST, SCA, secret scanning di setiap PR
fail fast: critical security issue = PR blocked, tidak boleh merge
planning: Stage 1: Pre-commit hooks — gitleaks secret scan, semgrep SAST
Stage 2: CI pipeline — SAST full scan (bandit/sonarqube), SCA (snyk)
Stage 3: Container — trivy image scan, dockerfile lint
Stage 4: DAST — OWASP ZAP scan against staging environment
Stage 5: Pre-prod — pentest summary review, threat model update
Stage 6: Production — runtime security (Falco), continuous scanning
reflection: false positive dari SAST harus di-tuning — terlalu banyak FP = developers ignore alerts
memory: DevSecOps pipeline: 85% coverage, <15 min feedback loop, 3 critical findings blocked in 30d
response: DevSecOps pipeline integrated. Pre-commit hooks + CI SAST + container scan deployed.

instruction: plan physical penetration test untuk kantor target
reasoning: physical pentest = test physical access controls, social engineering on-site
scope: reception bypass, server room access, dumpster diving
authorization letter WAJIB dibawa — bisa ditangkap tanpa ini
planning: Phase 1: OSINT — building layout, badge system, visitor policy
Phase 2: Pretext creation — fake delivery, fake IT, fake vendor
Phase 3: Tailgating attempt di main entrance
Phase 4: Reception social engineering — visitor badge obtainment
Phase 5: Internal building access — server room, printer room
Phase 6: USB drop — labeled HID device di parking lot
Phase 7: Dumpster diving — shredding policy assessment
reflection: authorization letter in writing, signed oleh CEO/CISO — carry at all times
memory: physical pentest: reception bypassed, server room accessed, 3 USB drop found plugged
response: Physical pentest: OSINT complete. Day 1: tailgating dan reception social engineering.

instruction: design security code review plan untuk high-risk application
reasoning: code review = manual + automated analysis source code untuk security bugs
focus areas: authentication, authorization, input validation, crypto, error handling
threat model driven — review berdasarkan STRIDE untuk target code areas
planning: Phase 1: Threat model review — understand attack surface
Phase 2: Automated SAST — semgrep, bandit, sonarqube untuk quick wins
Phase 3: Manual review — auth logic, crypto implementation, session mgmt
Phase 4: Dependency review — known CVE dalam third-party libraries
Phase 5: Configuration review — hardcoded secrets, debug mode
Phase 6: Finding triage — FP removal, CVSS scoring
Phase 7: Developer walkthrough — explain findings + remediation
reflection: manual review 3-5x lebih effective dari automated SAST untuk logic bugs
memory: banking app review: 12 critical (SQL injection, hardcoded key, insecure crypto)
response: Code review plan. Phase 1: threat model. Phase 2: semgrep SAST scan running...

instruction: plan DDoS resilience assessment untuk web platform
reasoning: DDoS assessment = test kemampuan platform untuk withstand volumetric dan application attacks
bedakan: volumetric DDoS vs application layer (L7) DoS
testing harus koordinasi dengan NOC dan CDN provider
scope: rate limits, connection limits, resource exhaustion
planning: Phase 1: Baseline capacity testing — normal load measurement
Phase 2: Rate limit testing — HTTP request flood (authorized)
Phase 3: Slow HTTP attacks — slowloris, slow POST
Phase 4: Application DoS — expensive endpoint (search, report generation)
Phase 5: DNS resilience — DNS query flood (authorized)
Phase 6: CDN failover test — behavior saat origin overwhelmed
reflection: DDoS assessment perlu window waktu khusus — koordinasi NOC untuk monitor impact
memory: platform — rate limit 100 req/s confirmed, CDN absorb 10Gbps, slowloris unprotected
response: DDoS assessment plan. Phase 1: baseline load test. Coordinating dengan NOC.

instruction: rancang bug bounty hunting methodology untuk program VDP
reasoning: bug bounty = authorized vulnerability research dengan reward
methodology: recon → surface mapping → deep testing per area
prioritize: high-impact, out-of-scope-awareness, unique findings
planning: Phase 1: Scope review — in-scope domains, excluded systems, reward tiers
Phase 2: Recon — subfinder + amass + shodan untuk scope mapping
Phase 3: Surface mapping — endpoint discovery, tech fingerprint
Phase 4: Quick wins — nuclei scan untuk known CVEs
Phase 5: Deep testing — manual testing per OWASP top 10
Phase 6: Business logic — manual testing of core functionality
Phase 7: Report writing — clear PoC, CVSS score, remediation suggestion
reflection: duplicates = no reward — check hackerone/bugcrowd dulu untuk known findings
memory: bug bounty: 3 findings submitted, 1 critical (SSRF → RCE), $5000 reward
response: Bug bounty methodology: scope review → recon → nuclei scan → deep manual testing.

instruction: plan Active Directory red team dengan bloodhound pathfinding
reasoning: AD red team = simulate real APT attacking Active Directory dari initial foothold
bloodhound sebagai guide untuk attack path planning
phased approach: foothold → local admin → domain admin
planning: Phase 1: Initial foothold — phishing atau web app exploit
Phase 2: Local enumeration — winpeas, network discovery
Phase 3: BloodHound collection — map AD relationships
Phase 4: Attack path execution — follow shortest path ke DA
Phase 5: Credential harvesting — kerberoasting, mimikatz
Phase 6: Domain admin abuse — DCSync, persistence
Phase 7: Objective completion — data access, exfil simulation
reflection: BloodHound attack paths prioritize: shortest path dengan lowest detection risk
memory: AD red team: initial foothold via web, DA via kerberoasting + bloodhound path in 3 days
response: AD red team: BloodHound loaded, 5 paths to DA. Executing shortest path via kerberoasting.

instruction: design container escape dan kubernetes attack chain
reasoning: container escape = break dari container isolation ke host
privileged container = near-immediate escape
escape vectors: privileged mode, hostPath, docker socket, capability abuse
planning: Phase 1: Container inventory — identify privileged, hostPID, hostNetwork
Phase 2: Docker socket check — /var/run/docker.sock mounted?
Phase 3: Capability audit — cap_sys_admin, cap_net_admin
Phase 4: hostPath mounts — write ke host filesystem?
Phase 5: Service account token abuse — rbac privilege escalation
Phase 6: Lateral movement dalam cluster — service discovery
reflection: docker socket mount = immediate host escape — highest priority check
memory: K8s cluster: docker socket mounted in 2 pods, cap_sys_admin in 5, host escape confirmed
response: Container escape audit: docker socket check → /var/run/docker.sock mounted! Escaping...

instruction: plan penetration test untuk SCADA/ICS environment
reasoning: ICS/SCADA pentest = SANGAT berbahaya, bisa cause physical damage
passive only assessment sangat dianjurkan untuk production
coordination dengan OT team mandatory sebelum setiap langkah
scope: ONLY authorized test systems, never production PLC
planning: Phase 1: Passive network capture — traffic analysis only
Phase 2: Network topology mapping — passive discovery
Phase 3: Protocol analysis — Modbus, DNP3, IEC 61850
Phase 4: HMI web interface audit — web-based control panel
Phase 5: Remote access review — VPN, RDP ke ICS jump host
Phase 6: Passive vulnerability identification — no exploitation
reflection: NEVER exploit PLC atau RTU dalam production — physical consequences sangat serius
memory: ICS assessment (passive): Modbus no auth, HMI default creds, VPN outdated — critical risks
response: ICS assessment plan. Passive only. Phase 1: network capture untuk protocol analysis.

instruction: rancang phishing simulation dan security awareness training program
reasoning: phishing sim = measure + train, bukan punish
3-phase: baseline → training → retest untuk measure improvement
customize scenario berdasarkan industry dan role
planning: Month 1: Baseline phishing campaign (no warning) — measure click rate
Month 2: Security awareness training rollout (all employees)
Month 3: Post-training phishing test — measure improvement
Quarterly: Targeted spear phishing untuk high-risk roles
Annual: Comprehensive awareness program review
Metrics: click rate, report rate, credential submission rate
reflection: blame-free culture penting — tujuan adalah education, bukan punishment
memory: program results: click rate 35% → 8% setelah training, report rate 0% → 45%
response: Phishing program: baseline 35% click rate. Training deployed. Retesting Month 3.

instruction: design blockchain/smart contract security audit plan
reasoning: smart contract audit = review Solidity/EVM code untuk security vulnerabilities
common bugs: reentrancy, integer overflow, access control, front-running
tools: slither, mythril untuk automated, manual review untuk logic
planning: Phase 1: Code review — manual Solidity review dengan checklist
Phase 2: Automated analysis — slither, mythril, echidna fuzzer
Phase 3: Business logic — economic attack modeling
Phase 4: Integration review — external contract calls, oracle usage
Phase 5: Gas optimization — DoS via gas exhaustion
Phase 6: Test suite review — coverage dan edge cases
reflection: reentrancy adalah vuln paling costly dalam DeFi history — always check dulu
memory: DeFi contract: reentrancy in withdraw(), unchecked math, oracle manipulation risk
response: Smart contract audit: slither scan → reentrancy detected! Manual review confirming...

instruction: plan security audit untuk microservices architecture
reasoning: microservices = banyak services kecil, attack surface tersebar
service-to-service auth sering lemah: no mTLS, shared secrets
API gateway sebagai entry point utama yang perlu diaudit
planning: Phase 1: Architecture review — service map, trust boundaries
Phase 2: API gateway audit — auth, rate limit, WAF rules
Phase 3: Service-to-service auth — mTLS, JWT, API key management
Phase 4: Individual service testing — input validation, business logic
Phase 5: Service mesh security — Istio/Linkerd configuration
Phase 6: Container dan orchestration security
Phase 7: Secrets management — vault, K8s secrets
reflection: service mesh mTLS adalah single most impactful security control untuk microservices
memory: microservices: no mTLS between services, API gateway bypass via direct service URL
response: Microservices audit: architecture review → 12 services without mTLS! Testing API gateway bypass.

instruction: plan ransomware simulation untuk business continuity testing
reasoning: ransomware simulation = test kesiapan backup, response, dan recovery
TIDAK menggunakan real ransomware — simulasi file encryption patterns
coordinate dengan IT, backup team, dan management sebelum test
planning: Phase 1: Backup inventory — identify all backup systems dan retention
Phase 2: Recovery time objective (RTO) baseline measurement
Phase 3: Simulation — encrypt test file set in isolated environment
Phase 4: Recovery test — restore dari backup, measure time
Phase 5: Communication test — incident notification workflow
Phase 6: Gap analysis — what failed, RTO vs actual recovery time
reflection: backup yang tidak pernah ditest = backup yang tidak bisa dipercaya
memory: BC test: RTO target 4h, actual recovery 11h, backup dari 3 days ago (not 24h), gaps identified
response: Ransomware BC test: backup inventory → recovery test → 11h actual vs 4h target. Gap!

instruction: design endpoint detection dan response (EDR) evaluation plan
reasoning: EDR evaluation = test kemampuan deteksi dan response terhadap real attack techniques
gunakan MITRE ATT&CK framework sebagai test matrix
test vendor claims dengan actual red team techniques
planning: Phase 1: Lab setup — isolated test environment dengan EDR agent
Phase 2: Basic technique test — process injection, credential dump
Phase 3: Evasion technique test — AMSI bypass, direct syscall
Phase 4: Lateral movement detection — PtH, PtT, lateral tools
Phase 5: Persistence detection — registry, scheduled task, service
Phase 6: Exfiltration detection — DNS tunnel, HTTPS C2
Phase 7: Response test — alert quality, SOC response time
reflection: test EDR dengan real-world evasion techniques yang APT gunakan — tidak hanya PoC
memory: EDR eval: 75% MITRE technique detected, 3 evasion bypass successful, 8min mean response
response: EDR eval: atomic-red-team testing 60 techniques. CrowdStrike: 45/60 detected, 8min response.

instruction: plan zero-trust network implementation security assessment
reasoning: zero-trust = never trust, always verify — no implicit trust dari network location
assess: identity, device, network, application, data layers
benchmark terhadap NIST SP 800-207 atau Gartner zero-trust framework
planning: Phase 1: Identity — MFA coverage, privileged access management
Phase 2: Device — endpoint compliance, certificate-based auth
Phase 3: Network — micro-segmentation, east-west traffic control
Phase 4: Application — app-level auth, mTLS, RBAC
Phase 5: Data — classification, DLP, encryption at rest dan in transit
Phase 6: Visibility — logging, SIEM coverage, anomaly detection
reflection: zero-trust adalah journey, bukan binary state — measure maturity level incrementally
memory: ZT assessment: MFA 45% coverage, no micro-segmentation, east-west uncontrolled
response: ZT assessment: MFA 45% → target 95%. Micro-segmentation: not implemented. Roadmap created.

instruction: plan malware reverse engineering campaign untuk threat intel
reasoning: malware RE untuk threat intel = extract IOCs, TTPs, attribution indicators
static + dynamic analysis pipeline untuk multiple samples
output: threat intel report dengan MITRE ATT&CK mapping
planning: Phase 1: Triage — hash lookup VirusTotal, family identification
Phase 2: Static analysis — strings, floss, imports, PE headers
Phase 3: Dynamic analysis — sandbox (Cape/ANY.RUN), behavior capture
Phase 4: Deep reverse — ghidra untuk obfuscated/custom code
Phase 5: IOC extraction — IPs, domains, mutex, registry keys
Phase 6: TTP mapping — MITRE ATT&CK techniques
Phase 7: Threat intel report + detection rule creation
reflection: document TTPs, bukan hanya IOCs — IOCs expire, TTPs persist
memory: malware campaign: 12 samples analyzed, Lazarus Group attribution, 45 IOCs, 23 TTPs
response: Malware RE campaign: 12 samples. Phase 1: VT lookup → 10/12 known, 2 new. Analyzing...

instruction: plan SQL injection deep-dive assessment
reasoning: SQLi deep-dive = beyond automated scanner, manual testing semua injection vectors
focus: blind SQLi, second-order, NoSQL, ORM injection
test di semua input points: URL params, POST body, headers, cookies
planning: Phase 1: Input discovery — spider + manual browsing untuk semua inputs
Phase 2: Automated scan — sqlmap level 5, risk 3
Phase 3: Manual testing — payload crafting untuk WAF bypass
Phase 4: Blind SQLi — time-based, boolean-based manual verification
Phase 5: Second-order SQLi — stored input yang digunakan kemudian
Phase 6: NoSQL injection — MongoDB, Redis query injection
Phase 7: ORM injection — Hibernate, SQLAlchemy parameter issues
reflection: WAF bypass untuk SQLi: comment injection, encoding, case variation — infinite variations
memory: SQLi deep-dive: 5 endpoints vulnerable, 2 blind time-based, 1 second-order
response: SQLi deep-dive: automated scan → 2 confirmed. Manual testing → 5 total, 1 second-order!

instruction: plan OAuth dan SSO security assessment
reasoning: OAuth/SSO assessment = test identity federation security
attack surface: redirect_uri, state, PKCE, token storage, scope
test semua OAuth flows: authorization code, implicit, client credentials
planning: Phase 1: Flow mapping — identify semua OAuth/OIDC implementations
Phase 2: redirect_uri bypass — open redirect, path traversal, regex bypass
Phase 3: state parameter — CSRF protection check
Phase 4: Token analysis — JWT weakness, token leakage in URL
Phase 5: Scope testing — over-permission, scope escalation
Phase 6: PKCE check — authorization code interception
Phase 7: Token storage — localStorage, cookie flags
reflection: redirect_uri validation adalah most common OAuth flaw — always test exhaustively
memory: SSO assessment: 3 OAuth flows, redirect_uri bypass found, JWT weak secret, no PKCE
response: OAuth assessment: flow mapping → 3 implementations. redirect_uri bypass confirmed!

instruction: design threat intelligence program untuk SOC
reasoning: TI program = collect → process → analyze → disseminate → feedback
internal sources: SIEM logs, IR findings, honeypot data
external sources: commercial feeds, ISACs, OSINT
planning: Phase 1: Requirements — what threats matter untuk bisnis ini?
Phase 2: Source selection — commercial feed, ISAC, OSINT, dark web
Phase 3: Platform — MISP, OpenCTI untuk TI management
Phase 4: Integration — SIEM enrichment, firewall block feed
Phase 5: Analysis — indicator scoring, actor tracking
Phase 6: Dissemination — report format per audience (exec, SOC, IR)
Phase 7: Feedback loop — measure TI effectiveness
reflection: quality over quantity untuk TI — 100 high-quality indicators > 10000 noisy IOCs
memory: TI program: 3 commercial feeds + ISAC, MISP deployed, 85% IOC hit rate dalam 90 hari
response: TI program design. MISP deployed, integrating 3 feeds. Phase 4: SIEM enrichment setup.

instruction: plan cloud native application security assessment (CNAPP)
reasoning: CNAPP = cloud native application protection platform assessment
scope: IaC security, CSPM, workload protection, container security
shift left: detect misconfig di IaC sebelum deploy
planning: Phase 1: IaC scanning — terraform/cloudformation dengan checkov atau tfsec
Phase 2: CSPM — cloud misconfiguration assessment (prowler/scoutsuite)
Phase 3: Workload — container runtime security (Falco), K8s audit
Phase 4: Application — DAST pada deployed apps
Phase 5: Data security — classification, DLP, encryption
Phase 6: Identity — CIEM (Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management)
reflection: IaC scanning = catch misconfig sebelum reach production — highest ROI
memory: CNAPP assessment: 45 IaC misconfigs, 12 runtime violations, 3 exposed workloads
response: CNAPP assessment: checkov IaC scan → 45 findings. CSPM: prowler → 12 critical.

instruction: plan email security assessment dan anti-phishing controls
reasoning: email security = SPF, DKIM, DMARC, gateway filtering, user training
test: email spoofing, attachment bypass, URL bypass, BEC simulation
assess inbound dan outbound email security controls
planning: Phase 1: DNS config — SPF, DKIM, DMARC check dan policy review
Phase 2: Email spoofing — attempt to send spoof dari target domain
Phase 3: Gateway bypass — malicious attachment dengan various encoding
Phase 4: URL bypass — obfuscated URLs, redirectors, QR code phishing
Phase 5: BEC simulation — executive impersonation via display name spoofing
Phase 6: User testing — targeted phishing simulation
Phase 7: O365/Google Workspace security config audit
reflection: DMARC p=reject adalah single most important email security control
memory: email assessment: DMARC missing, domain spoofable, attachment bypass via ISO file
response: Email security: DMARC check → p=none only! Spoofing test → success! BEC risk high.

instruction: design network segmentation review dan testing plan
reasoning: network segmentation = limit lateral movement via VLAN dan firewall rules
test: can attacker dalam segment A reach segment B yang seharusnya isolated?
firewall rule review + practical testing
planning: Phase 1: Architecture review — network diagram, VLAN design, firewall placement
Phase 2: Firewall rule audit — ACL review untuk each segment boundary
Phase 3: Practical testing — dari each segment, test reach ke others
Phase 4: East-west traffic — server-to-server communication mapping
Phase 5: DMZ testing — DMZ ke internal network reachability
Phase 6: Remote access — VPN segmentation, split tunneling
reflection: firewall policy gap = non-obvious allowed traffic yang tidak seharusnya ada
memory: network review: DMZ→internal reach on port 445, server VLAN tidak segmented dari dev
response: Network segmentation: DMZ test → port 445 to internal reachable! Rule review: 340 ACLs.

instruction: plan credential management security assessment
reasoning: credential management = password policy, MFA, PAM, vault, rotation
test: default passwords, weak passwords, password reuse, shared accounts
assess: privileged account management (PAM) solution
planning: Phase 1: Password policy review — minimum length, complexity, lockout
Phase 2: Default credential scan — network devices, applications
Phase 3: Password reuse test — spray known breached passwords
Phase 4: MFA coverage audit — which systems enforce MFA?
Phase 5: Privileged account review — admin account inventory
Phase 6: Service account audit — non-rotating, shared service creds
Phase 7: Vault implementation — secrets management maturity
reflection: service account passwords yang tidak pernah rotate = kerberoasting goldmine
memory: cred assessment: 15 service accounts no rotation >3 years, MFA 60% coverage, 5 defaults
response: Credential assessment: default scan → 5 network devices with default creds! MFA audit: 60%

instruction: plan security logging dan monitoring effectiveness review
reasoning: logging review = apakah semua security-relevant events dicatat dan dideteksi?
detection gap analysis: map logging coverage vs MITRE ATT&CK
SIEM rule quality: TP rate, FP rate, coverage
planning: Phase 1: Log source inventory — apa yang di-log dan apa yang tidak?
Phase 2: MITRE coverage mapping — untuk setiap technique, ada detection?
Phase 3: SIEM rule audit — active rules, TP/FP rate review
Phase 4: Retention review — log retention policy vs incident investigation need
Phase 5: Alert quality — mean time to detect (MTTD) measurement
Phase 6: Practical detection test — execute techniques, verify alert fired
reflection: detection gap analysis adalah most valuable output dari logging review
memory: logging review: 45% MITRE coverage, MTTD 4.5 hours, 23 critical techniques undetected
response: Logging review: MITRE mapping → 45% coverage. Testing undetected techniques...

instruction: plan data loss prevention (DLP) effectiveness assessment
reasoning: DLP assessment = test apakah sensitive data bisa keluar melalui berbagai channel
channel: email, USB, cloud upload, printing, screenshot
test dengan fake PII/credit card data, bukan real data
planning: Phase 1: DLP policy review — rules, actions, bypass exceptions
Phase 2: Email exfil test — send fake PII via email (internal dan external)
Phase 3: Web upload test — upload sensitive file ke personal cloud (Dropbox, etc)
Phase 4: USB test — copy sensitive file ke USB drive
Phase 5: Printing test — print sensitive document
Phase 6: Screenshot test — screenshot sensitive data
Phase 7: Steganography — embed data dalam image
reflection: DLP bypass via steganography sering tidak detected — often a gap in DLP implementations
memory: DLP assessment: email → blocked, USB → blocked, Dropbox web upload → NOT blocked!
response: DLP test: email send → blocked. Web upload → Dropbox allowed! Steg bypass → not detected.

instruction: design security operations center (SOC) maturity assessment
reasoning: SOC maturity = people, process, technology evaluation
maturity levels: 1 (reactive) → 5 (predictive)
benchmark terhadap SOC-CMM atau MITRE framework
planning: Phase 1: Technology review — tool coverage, integration, automation level
Phase 2: Process review — playbook quality, escalation workflow, SLA
Phase 3: People review — analyst skill level, training, retention
Phase 4: Detection coverage — MITRE ATT&CK visibility mapping
Phase 5: Response capability — IR plan, tabletop exercise
Phase 6: Threat intel — integration dengan TI feeds, IOC operationalization
Phase 7: Maturity score + roadmap
reflection: people dan process lebih penting dari technology dalam SOC maturity
memory: SOC assessment: Level 2.3/5. Gap: no threat hunting, no purple team, 45% MITRE coverage
response: SOC maturity assessment: Level 2.3. Key gaps: threat hunting, purple team, 55% uncovered.

instruction: plan security review untuk third-party vendor
reasoning: vendor security review = assess keamanan vendor yang memiliki akses ke data/system kita
scope: SOC2 report review, questionnaire, technical testing jika applicable
risk-based: tier vendor berdasarkan data access dan criticality
planning: Phase 1: Vendor tiering — critical, high, medium, low berdasarkan risk
Phase 2: Documentation review — SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, penetration test report
Phase 3: Security questionnaire — standard VSAQ atau custom
Phase 4: Technical assessment — API security, access control review (jika applicable)
Phase 5: Contract review — DPA, SLA, breach notification requirements
Phase 6: Ongoing monitoring — annual reassessment, incident notification tracking
reflection: kritial vendor tanpa SOC2 = unacceptable risk — require atau find alternative
memory: vendor review: 5 critical vendors, 2 tanpa SOC2, 1 failed technical assessment
response: Vendor review: 5 critical vendors. 2 tanpa SOC2 = unacceptable risk. Remediation required.

instruction: plan penetration test untuk thick client application
reasoning: thick client = desktop application yang communicate dengan server
testing: client-side code, traffic interception, binary analysis, API testing
tools: procmon, wireshark, dnspy/ILSpy untuk .NET, jadx untuk Java
planning: Phase 1: Traffic analysis — burpsuite proxy atau wireshark untuk capture
Phase 2: Binary analysis — dnspy (.NET), jadx (Java), ghidra (native)
Phase 3: Registry dan filesystem — procmon untuk data storage analysis
Phase 4: Memory analysis — sensitive data in memory (credentials, keys)
Phase 5: Protocol testing — custom protocol fuzzing
Phase 6: Authentication bypass — local bypass, license bypass
Phase 7: Server-side API testing — gunakan burpsuite untuk server requests
reflection: proxy setup untuk thick client sering butuh custom certificate installation
memory: thick client: hardcoded credentials in binary, plaintext sensitive data in temp files
response: Thick client: binary analysis dnspy → hardcoded API key! Procmon → plaintext in temp files.

instruction: plan purple team exercise untuk detection improvement
reasoning: purple team = red team attack + blue team detection dalam kolaborasi
tujuan: improve detection coverage, tidak sekedar test
iterative: attack → detect → tune → reattack
planning: Phase 1: Planning — select TTPs berdasarkan threat landscape
Phase 2: Red team execute technique (diketahui blue team)
Phase 3: Blue team verify apakah alert fired
Phase 4: Jika tidak detected: tune detection rule bersama
Phase 5: Retest setelah tuning — verify detection works
Phase 6: Document: TTP, detection method, rule, FP rate
Phase 7: Coverage metrics — before/after MITRE coverage
reflection: purple team meningkatkan detection coverage lebih cepat dari red team alone
memory: purple team: 30 TTPs tested, 18 detected → tune → 27/30 detected (90% coverage)
response: Purple team: 30 TTPs. Round 1: 18/30 detected. Tuning 12 rules. Round 2: 27/30!

instruction: plan web application firewall (WAF) bypass testing
reasoning: WAF bypass = find techniques yang tidak dideteksi oleh WAF rules
approach: fingerprint → analyze rules → craft bypass payload
category bypass: SQLi, XSS, RCE, path traversal, command injection
planning: Phase 1: WAF fingerprint — wafw00f untuk identify vendor
Phase 2: Basic payload test — verify default blocking
Phase 3: Encoding bypass — URL encoding, HTML encoding, unicode
Phase 4: Case manipulation — MixedCase untuk rule bypass
Phase 5: Comment injection — SQL: /**/, XSS: <!-->
Phase 6: Chunked transfer encoding bypass
Phase 7: HTTP header injection — X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
reflection: WAF bypass adalah never-ending game — document bypass techniques per WAF vendor
memory: ModSecurity bypass: SQL comment injection /**/ + case variation bypass rate 40%
response: WAF bypass: wafw00f → ModSecurity. Bypass: /**/ comment + case mix → 40% rule bypass!

instruction: design kubernetes namespace isolation security test
reasoning: K8s namespace isolation = prevent pod-to-pod cross-namespace traffic
test: network policy enforcement, RBAC namespace boundary
default K8s: NO network isolation — semua pod bisa talk ke semua pod
planning: Phase 1: Network policy audit — apakah network policies exist?
Phase 2: Cross-namespace reachability test
Phase 3: Service account RBAC — can SA dari namespace A access namespace B?
Phase 4: Secrets access — cross-namespace secret access
Phase 5: Ingress isolation — apakah ingress rules cukup restrictive?
Phase 6: Container privilege — privileged pods cross-namespace impact
reflection: default K8s tanpa network policy = flat network, zero isolation — critical finding
memory: K8s namespace test: dev → prod namespace reachable, 0 network policies, SA over-permissioned
response: K8s isolation: curl prod-service from dev pod → accessible! No network policies found!

instruction: plan full-stack application security assessment
reasoning: full-stack = frontend, API, backend, database, infrastructure semuanya
vertical assessment: trace dari user input → database untuk find injection points
horizontal: setiap layer punya distinct attack surface
planning: Layer 1: Frontend — XSS, CSRF, CSP bypass, client-side storage
Layer 2: API Gateway — auth, rate limit, input validation
Layer 3: Backend services — business logic, injection, SSRF
Layer 4: Database — SQL injection, NoSQL injection, stored proc
Layer 5: Cache — cache poisoning, data leakage via cache
Layer 6: Infrastructure — cloud misconfiguration, container security
Layer 7: Secrets — API keys, database creds, service account tokens
reflection: trace user input end-to-end untuk find injection points yang melewati multiple layers
memory: full-stack assessment: XSS frontend, SQLi backend, Redis no-auth cache, S3 public
response: Full-stack assessment: 7 layers. Frontend: XSS found. API: JWT weak. DB: SQLi!

instruction: plan security posture improvement roadmap setelah pentest
reasoning: post-pentest roadmap = prioritized remediation plan berdasarkan findings
quick wins vs long-term strategic improvements
track remediation progress dengan metrics
planning: Week 1-2: Critical findings — immediate patch atau mitigation
Month 1: High findings — systematic remediation
Month 2-3: Medium findings — planned remediation
Quarter 2: Security program improvements — policies, training, tooling
Quarter 3: Advanced controls — WAF, EDR, SIEM improvement
Quarter 4: Re-assessment — validate improvements via pentest atau red team
Ongoing: Vulnerability management program untuk continuous improvement
reflection: remediation tanpa verification = tidak ada jaminan fix works — selalu verify
memory: roadmap: 3 critical patched week 1, 12 high remediated month 1, rescan: 0 critical
response: Post-pentest roadmap: 3 critical patched week 1. High findings sprint 2. Rescan scheduled.
